steps.title: Turn the g table into an f table
steps.content:
The table gives $g(x)$, but the question asks for the y-intercept of $y=f(x)$. Since $g(x)=\dfrac{f(x)}{x+2}$, multiply each table output by $x+2$ to get $f(x)$:
  • $f(-14)=1(-14+2)=-12$
  • $f(-8)=0(-8+2)=0$
  • $f(10)=3(10+2)=36$

steps.title: Find the linear function f
steps.content: Use two of the converted points, $(-8,0)$ and $(10,36)$. The slope is $$m=\frac{36-0}{10-(-8)}=\frac{36}{18}=2.$$ Since $f(-8)=0$, use $f(x)=2x+b$:$$0=2(-8)+b\Rightarrow b=16$$So $f(x)=2x+16$.

steps.title: Identify the intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept of $y=f(x)$ is where $x=0$. From $f(x)=2x+16$, the intercept is $(0,16)$. D is correct.
